channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump unveils $20 billion investment in American data centers
item.description: U.S. President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced a $20 billion new investment to build data centers across the United States. At a news conference at his home in Palm Beach, Florida, Trump said the investor is Emirati billionaire Hussain Sajwani, chairman of DAMAC properties.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals
item.description: Outgoing National Cyber Director Harry Coker says ONCD could also lead more efforts governmentwide on cybersecurity planning and response. The post WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ Amazon pays $40 million for Melania Trump documentary
item.description: The choice by Bezos's film and TV studio to pay such a sum to produce Melania Trump's 'vanity documentary' represents the latest public effort by the Amazon founder to "warm up" to Trump after years of feuding, media observers say.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: SAP S/4HANA Workloads on IBM Power Systems: A New Era in Cloud Migration
item.description: SAP S/4HANA Workloads on IBM Power Systems: A New Era in Cloud Migration
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The post Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Almost nothing remains of Software AG
item.description: Software AG (now legally known as Software GmbH) has sold its Alfabet and Cumulocity divisions, following on from the recent sales of webMethods, StreamSets and TrendMiner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google fires back as Microsoft is accused of 'tricking' people into using Bing
item.description: Google vs Microsoft fight gets ugly as Chrome boss accuses company of 'spoofing the Google homepage'.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Emirati Billionaire to Invest $20 Billion in US Data Centers
item.description: President-elect Trump announces $20 billion investment from Emirati billionaire Hussain Sajwani for data centers across the US
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: CES 2025 day 2: the 11 coolest gadgets we've seen, from Samsung's The Frame Pro TV to AI air fryers
item.description: CES 2025 is continuing in Las Vegas, and the TechRadar team is busy picking out the highlights of the show.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI potential meets ROI pragmatism: 3 crucial questions every CIO should ask
item.description: If you had to name 2023’s single-most impactful and disruptive technology, you’d need just two letters: AI. With the release of OpenAI’s ChatGPT in November 2022, we watched a tsunami of AI news and noise throughout the year. And there’s no sign of things slowing down.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Last of Us season 2's new teaser has fans gushing over every detail it contains, but some are also concerned about the welfare of one of its new cast members
item.description: The Last of Us season 2's latest round of footage is full of moments lifted directly from Naughty Dog's video game franchise.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Maingear's newest PC builds are so good I wish I could buy them all, but I'll happily take the Miami Vice one, thanks
item.description: Maingear's latest custom-built gaming PCs are on display at CES 2025, and trust me, you're going to want to see them in person.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Think Oura and Samsung's smart rings look good? Ultrahuman just unveiled its Ultrahuman Rare 18K gold and platinum luxury smart rings at CES 2025
item.description: Oura and Samsung rival Ultrahuman has launched smart rings made from real 18K gold and platinum at CES.
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Go teams struggle with coding standards – survey
item.description: Although developer sentiment toward the Go language remains very positive, teams face a challenge when it comes to maintaining consistent coding standards across Go code bases, according to Go developer survey results for the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World
